start: ^Welcome to your first day on the job. I'm your boss and I'll be helping you get situated.
^Looks like you already found the map! This is your assigned neighborhood for today.
^Your job is to keep the vampires in check while they do their rounds. You're part of the watch team, so when you see something illegal happening it's your duty to report it.
^You'll be able to see vampires roam around in real time. When an interaction starts between a vampire and a villager, you'll see a "!" on the map.
^Oh, looks like something is happening right now. I'll walk you through your first interaction.
^Use the mouse to click on the "!" and the map will automatically zoom in for you to watch.
^You can see the vampire at the door right? It's very common for a vampire to try and trick people into letting them into their private property.
^Let's see what this one tries to do.
^According to vampire code, if they do not have consent or a legal warrant to search, then they cannot enter private property.
^Now we're at the part of your job where you have to make a choice.
^Down there is the report function. When you are watching an interaction, you can report the involved vampire at any time. Don't use it if the vampire doesn't break code.

^We use these reports to keep track of violations from vampires; if you falsely report someone it could mess up our data. We want to eventually change some of the outdated rules in the vampire code, so we need it to be as accurate as possible.
^Back to the situation: the choice you need to make should be clear. Go ahead and report the vampire.

^Unfortunately, we can't actively intervene when we see an illegal scenario like this taking place. It sucks— but that's why we are trying to change the vampire code: to help protect these villagers from being wronged.
^Keep your eyes peeled. I'm hopeful that you can get some good work done for the community. I think you can handle the rest on your own for today.

^Oh, almost forgot.. incidents don't wait for you to finish the previous one. If another situation appears which might require your action, you'll see it on the minimap. You can click the minimap to go back into the full map view.
^You may need to juggle more than one scenario at a time. Just remember that all documents you have access to are only for the situation you're focused on. I trust you won't confuse them.

^If you leave a scenario before it ends to check on another one, don't worry. You will still be able to go back to it, but remember that it's in real-time; things may happen while you're not watching.
